V33 Analysis: Cross-Channel Attention in FAM

ST-JEPA Collapse Confirmed; Channel Dropout Within Noise

Published

April 27, 2026

Overview

V33 tested two cross-channel attention strategies against a matched-protocol FAM baseline on PSM, SMAP, and FD001. The headline finding: ST-JEPA (per-channel tokenization + factored temporal/cross-channel attention) collapses within epoch 1 of pretraining on all three datasets, confirming the v14 and v22 failure pattern. Channel dropout produced no statistically significant improvement.

Code 
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import json
from pathlib import Path

# Colorblind-safe palette (tab10 subset)
COLORS = {
    'baseline': '#1f77b4',   # blue
    'ch_drop':  '#2ca02c',   # green
    'stjepa':   '#d62728',   # red
    'v30_ref':  '#7f7f7f',   # gray
}

DATASETS = ['PSM', 'SMAP', 'FD001']

# All numbers from phase JSONs
data = {
    'PSM': {
        'v30_ref':      0.562,
        'baseline':     {'mean': 0.5545, 'std': 0.0290, 'seeds': [0.5436, 0.5874, 0.5325]},
        'ch_drop':      {'mean': 0.5678, 'std': 0.0037, 'seeds': [0.5681, 0.5713, 0.5639], 'rate': 0.5},
        'stjepa':       {'mean': 0.4787, 'std': 0.0117, 'seeds': [0.4871, 0.4835, 0.4653], 'mask': 0.6},
        'stat_cd': {'t': 0.896, 'p': 0.465, 'd': 0.457, 'sig': False},
        'stat_st': {'t': -5.289, 'p': 0.034, 'd': -2.616, 'sig': True},
    },
    'SMAP': {
        'v30_ref':      0.598,
        'baseline':     {'mean': 0.5324, 'std': 0.0966, 'seeds': [0.5262, 0.6319, 0.4390]},
        'ch_drop':      {'mean': 0.5767, 'std': 0.0359, 'seeds': [0.6169, 0.5477, 0.5656], 'rate': 0.0},
        'stjepa':       {'mean': 0.4892, 'std': 0.0146, 'seeds': [0.5059, 0.4828, 0.4789], 'mask': 0.0},
        'stat_cd': {'t': 0.681, 'p': 0.566, 'd': 0.459, 'sig': False},
        'stat_st': {'t': -0.775, 'p': 0.520, 'd': -0.447, 'sig': False},
    },
    'FD001': {
        'v30_ref':      0.786,
        'baseline':     {'mean': 0.7208, 'std': 0.0560, 'seeds': [0.7534, 0.6562, 0.7529]},
        'ch_drop':      {'mean': 0.7322, 'std': 0.0165, 'seeds': [0.7344, 0.7476, 0.7148], 'rate': 0.1},
        'stjepa':       {'mean': 0.4940, 'std': 0.0324, 'seeds': [0.4688, 0.4827, 0.5306], 'mask': 0.0},
        'stat_cd': {'t': 0.284, 'p': 0.803, 'd': 0.204, 'sig': False},
        'stat_st': {'t': -7.056, 'p': 0.020, 'd': -4.052, 'sig': True},
    },
}

Headline Table

Code 
from IPython.display import display, HTML

rows = []
for ds in DATASETS:
    d = data[ds]
    b = d['baseline']
    c = d['ch_drop']
    s = d['stjepa']
    ref = d['v30_ref']

    # Find winner (numeric, not statistical)
    winner = max(['baseline', 'ch_drop', 'stjepa'], key=lambda k: data[ds][k]['mean'])

    def fmt(m, std, is_winner=False):
        s_str = f"{m:.4f} +/- {std:.4f}"
        return f"<b>{s_str}</b>" if is_winner else s_str

    rows.append(f"""
    <tr>
        <td>{ds}</td>
        <td>{ref:.3f}</td>
        <td>{fmt(b['mean'], b['std'], winner=='baseline')}</td>
        <td>{fmt(c['mean'], c['std'], winner=='ch_drop')} (rate={c['rate']})</td>
        <td>{fmt(s['mean'], s['std'], winner=='stjepa')} (mask={s['mask']})</td>
    </tr>""")

html = f"""
<table border="1" style="border-collapse:collapse; font-size:14px; margin:10px 0">
<thead>
  <tr style="background:#f0f0f0">
    <th>Dataset</th><th>v30 Ref</th><th>Baseline v33</th>
    <th>Ch-Drop (best rate)</th><th>ST-JEPA (best mask)</th>
  </tr>
</thead>
<tbody>{''.join(rows)}</tbody>
</table>
<p style="font-size:12px; color:#666">h-AUROC mean +/- std (n=3 seeds). <b>Bold</b> = numeric winner per row.
Ch-drop wins are not statistically significant (all p > 0.45).
ST-JEPA significantly worse on PSM (p=0.034) and FD001 (p=0.020).</p>
"""
display(HTML(html))
Dataset v30 Ref Baseline v33 Ch-Drop (best rate) ST-JEPA (best mask)
PSM 0.562 0.5545 +/- 0.0290 0.5678 +/- 0.0037 (rate=0.5) 0.4787 +/- 0.0117 (mask=0.6)
SMAP 0.598 0.5324 +/- 0.0966 0.5767 +/- 0.0359 (rate=0.0) 0.4892 +/- 0.0146 (mask=0.0)
FD001 0.786 0.7208 +/- 0.0560 0.7322 +/- 0.0165 (rate=0.1) 0.4940 +/- 0.0324 (mask=0.0)

h-AUROC mean +/- std (n=3 seeds). Bold = numeric winner per row. Ch-drop wins are not statistically significant (all p > 0.45). ST-JEPA significantly worse on PSM (p=0.034) and FD001 (p=0.020).

Bar Chart: h-AUROC by Dataset and Variant

Code 
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 5), sharey=False)
fig.suptitle("V33 Cross-Channel Ablation: h-AUROC by Dataset", fontsize=14, y=1.02)

variants = [
    ('baseline', 'Baseline v33', COLORS['baseline']),
    ('ch_drop',  'Ch-Drop',      COLORS['ch_drop']),
    ('stjepa',   'ST-JEPA',      COLORS['stjepa']),
]

for ax, ds in zip(axes, DATASETS):
    d = data[ds]
    xs = np.arange(len(variants))
    means = [d[v]['mean'] for v, _, _ in variants]
    stds  = [d[v]['std']  for v, _, _ in variants]
    labels = [lbl for _, lbl, _ in variants]
    cols   = [c   for _, _, c  in variants]

    bars = ax.bar(xs, means, color=cols, alpha=0.85, width=0.55,
                  yerr=stds, capsize=5, error_kw={'linewidth': 1.5})

    # v30 reference line
    ax.axhline(d['v30_ref'], color=COLORS['v30_ref'], linestyle='--',
               linewidth=1.2, label=f"v30 ref ({d['v30_ref']:.3f})")

    # Annotate p-values
    ax.text(1, means[1] + stds[1] + 0.01,
            f"p={d['stat_cd']['p']:.3f}", ha='center', fontsize=8, color=COLORS['ch_drop'])
    pval_st = d['stat_st']['p']
    sig_marker = '*' if pval_st < 0.05 else ''
    ax.text(2, means[2] + stds[2] + 0.01,
            f"p={pval_st:.3f}{sig_marker}", ha='center', fontsize=8, color=COLORS['stjepa'])

    ax.set_title(ds, fontsize=13)
    ax.set_xticks(xs)
    ax.set_xticklabels(labels, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel("h-AUROC", fontsize=10)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.set_ylim(bottom=max(0, min(means) - max(stds) - 0.12))
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FormatStrFormatter('%.3f'))
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("v33_ablation_bar.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Per-Seed Breakdown

Code 
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 5), sharey=False)
fig.suptitle("V33 Per-Seed h-AUROC", fontsize=14, y=1.02)

seed_labels = ['s42', 's123', 's456']
jitter = [-0.12, 0.0, 0.12]

for ax, ds in zip(axes, DATASETS):
    d = data[ds]
    for j, (vkey, vlabel, col) in enumerate(variants):
        seeds = d[vkey]['seeds']
        xs = [j + ji for ji in jitter]
        ax.scatter(xs, seeds, color=col, label=vlabel, s=60, zorder=3, alpha=0.9)
        # Connect seeds with thin lines
        for xi, yi in zip(xs, seeds):
            ax.plot([xi], [yi], 'o', color=col, markersize=8, alpha=0.9)
        # Mean line
        ax.hlines(d[vkey]['mean'], j - 0.18, j + 0.18, colors=col,
                  linewidth=2.5, linestyle='-', alpha=0.6)

    ax.axhline(d['v30_ref'], color=COLORS['v30_ref'], linestyle='--',
               linewidth=1.2, label=f"v30 ref")

    ax.set_title(ds, fontsize=13)
    ax.set_xticks([0, 1, 2])
    ax.set_xticklabels(['Baseline', 'Ch-Drop', 'ST-JEPA'], fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel("h-AUROC", fontsize=10)
    if ds == 'PSM':
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("v33_per_seed.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Statistical Test Summary

Code 
rows_stat = []
for ds in DATASETS:
    d = data[ds]
    for comp, key, method in [
        ('Ch-drop vs Baseline', 'stat_cd', f"rate={data[ds]['ch_drop']['rate']}"),
        ('ST-JEPA vs Baseline', 'stat_st', f"mask={data[ds]['stjepa']['mask']}"),
    ]:
        st = d[key]
        sig_str = "<span style='color:red;font-weight:bold'>YES</span>" if st['sig'] else "No"
        delta = data[ds]['ch_drop']['mean'] - data[ds]['baseline']['mean'] if 'cd' in key else \
                data[ds]['stjepa']['mean'] - data[ds]['baseline']['mean']
        rows_stat.append(f"""
        <tr>
            <td>{ds}</td>
            <td>{comp} ({method})</td>
            <td>{delta:+.4f}</td>
            <td>{st['t']:.3f}</td>
            <td>{st['p']:.3f}</td>
            <td>{st['d']:.3f}</td>
            <td>{sig_str}</td>
        </tr>""")

html_stat = f"""
<table border="1" style="border-collapse:collapse; font-size:13px; margin:10px 0">
<thead>
  <tr style="background:#f0f0f0">
    <th>Dataset</th><th>Comparison</th><th>Delta</th>
    <th>t</th><th>p (two-sided)</th><th>Cohen's d</th><th>p &lt; 0.05?</th>
  </tr>
</thead>
<tbody>{''.join(rows_stat)}</tbody>
</table>
<p style="font-size:12px;color:#666">Paired t-test, n=3 seeds. With n=3, only |d| > 2.0 reaches p < 0.05.
ST-JEPA produces very large negative effects on PSM (d=-2.62) and FD001 (d=-4.05),
confirming that collapse is not noise.</p>
"""
display(HTML(html_stat))
Dataset Comparison Delta t p (two-sided) Cohen's d p < 0.05?
PSM Ch-drop vs Baseline (rate=0.5) +0.0133 0.896 0.465 0.457 No
PSM ST-JEPA vs Baseline (mask=0.6) -0.0758 -5.289 0.034 -2.616 YES
SMAP Ch-drop vs Baseline (rate=0.0) +0.0443 0.681 0.566 0.459 No
SMAP ST-JEPA vs Baseline (mask=0.0) -0.0432 -0.775 0.520 -0.447 No
FD001 Ch-drop vs Baseline (rate=0.1) +0.0114 0.284 0.803 0.204 No
FD001 ST-JEPA vs Baseline (mask=0.0) -0.2268 -7.056 0.020 -4.052 YES

Paired t-test, n=3 seeds. With n=3, only |d| > 2.0 reaches p < 0.05. ST-JEPA produces very large negative effects on PSM (d=-2.62) and FD001 (d=-4.05), confirming that collapse is not noise.

Channel Dropout Sweep (Seed 42 Only)

Code 
sweep_data = {
    'PSM': {
        'rates': [0.0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5],
        'auroc': [0.5787, 0.5607, 0.5584, 0.5834],
    },
    'SMAP': {
        'rates': [0.0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5],
        'auroc': [0.6301, 0.5652, 0.5242, 0.4048],
    },
    'FD001': {
        'rates': [0.0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5],
        'auroc': [0.7216, 0.7369, 0.7289, 0.7307],
    },
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 4), sharey=False)
fig.suptitle("Channel Dropout Rate Sweep (seed 42)", fontsize=13, y=1.02)

for ax, ds in zip(axes, DATASETS):
    d = sweep_data[ds]
    ax.plot(d['rates'], d['auroc'], 'o-', color=COLORS['ch_drop'],
            linewidth=2, markersize=8)
    # Mark best
    best_idx = np.argmax(d['auroc'])
    ax.scatter([d['rates'][best_idx]], [d['auroc'][best_idx]],
               color='black', s=100, zorder=5, label=f"best=rate {d['rates'][best_idx]}")
    # Phase 1 baseline (seed 42 only)
    ax.axhline(data[ds]['baseline']['seeds'][0], color=COLORS['baseline'],
               linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, label='baseline s42')
    ax.set_title(ds, fontsize=12)
    ax.set_xlabel("Channel dropout rate", fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("h-AUROC", fontsize=10)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("v33_ch_drop_sweep.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

ST-JEPA Mask Ratio Sweep (Seed 42 Only)

Code 
stjepa_sweep = {
    'PSM': {
        'ratios': [0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6],
        'auroc':  [0.4674, 0.4752, 0.4776, 0.4871],
    },
    'SMAP': {
        'ratios': [0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6],
        'auroc':  [0.5059, 0.5044, 0.4932, 0.4981],
    },
    'FD001': {
        'ratios': [0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6],
        'auroc':  [0.4688, 0.4493, 0.4636, 0.4571],
    },
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 4), sharey=False)
fig.suptitle("ST-JEPA Channel Mask Ratio Sweep (seed 42)", fontsize=13, y=1.02)

for ax, ds in zip(axes, DATASETS):
    d = stjepa_sweep[ds]
    ax.plot(d['ratios'], d['auroc'], 's-', color=COLORS['stjepa'],
            linewidth=2, markersize=8)
    # Phase 1 baseline (seed 42 only)
    ax.axhline(data[ds]['baseline']['seeds'][0], color=COLORS['baseline'],
               linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, label='baseline s42')
    # Annotate range
    lo, hi = min(d['auroc']), max(d['auroc'])
    ax.annotate(f"range: {hi-lo:.4f}", xy=(0.05, 0.05),
                xycoords='axes fraction', fontsize=9, color='gray')
    ax.set_title(ds, fontsize=12)
    ax.set_xlabel("Channel mask ratio", fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("h-AUROC", fontsize=10)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("v33_stjepa_sweep.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Collapse Diagnostic: ST-JEPA vs Baseline Representation Quality

The sweep above shows that increasing the channel mask ratio from 0.0 to 0.6 changes the ST-JEPA AUROC by only 0.010-0.020 (essentially nothing). This is the representational collapse signature: the encoder has learned to produce a near-constant output regardless of input, so mask ratio only modulates noise.

Key diagnostic values from the COLLAPSED guard check (logged during pretraining): - h_std (representation std across batch) should be ~0.10-0.20 for a healthy encoder - h_std for ST-JEPA at epoch 1: ~0.003 (all datasets, all mask ratios) - val_loss for ST-JEPA at epoch 1: ~0.005 (trivially low - encoder outputs constant, predictor trivially wins)

Compare to baseline pretraining: - h_std for baseline encoder (end of training): approximately 0.10-0.15 - val_loss for baseline: 0.010-0.028 (still positive, encoder is non-trivial)

The ST-JEPA collapse is categorical - not a marginal degradation. The encoder stops learning after the first few gradient steps.

Internal Consistency Check

Per the project’s internal-consistency protocol, every positive result requires cross-artifact verification. Here we document the (negative) result’s consistency:

Artifact Value Consistent?
ST-JEPA PSM h-AUROC 0.479 +/- 0.012 Yes - consistent with collapse
ST-JEPA FD001 h-AUROC 0.494 +/- 0.032 Yes - near chance, consistent with collapse
COLLAPSED guard trigger epoch 1, h_std=0.003 Yes - explains h-AUROC ~0.48-0.50
Sweep flatness (range < 0.02) ST-JEPA all datasets Yes - mask ratio irrelevant when collapsed
Ch-drop PSM p=0.465 Not significant Yes - delta 0.013 < 1 sigma baseline
SMAP best rate=0.0 No dropout wins Yes - consistent with no channel-dropout signal

All artifacts tell a consistent story: ST-JEPA collapses, channel-dropout is noise.

Key Takeaway

The channel-fusion patch embedding (Linear(C x patch_size, d_model)) is architecturally necessary for stable FAM pretraining. Per-channel tokenization (Linear(patch_size, d_model) shared across channels) allows the encoder to trivially satisfy the EMA prediction objective by encoding each channel’s mean as a fixed direction, without learning temporal structure. This failure mode persists even when: - Learnable channel embeddings are absent (anti-v14 safeguard) - Channel masking is applied during pretraining (0.0 - 0.6 sweep) - Multiple datasets with different cross-channel correlation structures are tested

The negative result is documented as a methodological note supporting the main FAM architecture design.
